office_address: still 123 Main Street, Springfield, New York 10001
emergency_definition: - no heat when the outside temperature is below 45 degrees - gas smell - sparking or smoke coming from equipment
emergency_routing_rules: - During business hours, emergency calls should be transferred directly to the on-call technician's mobile at 555-111-2222. - After hours, emergency calls should first try that same technician number.
non_emergency_routing_rules: - During business hours, if the office line is available, transfer to the main office line at 555-000-1234. - If no one answers, take a message and summarize the issue. - After hours, always take a message for non-emergencies and mark it as next-business-day follow-up.
call_transfer_rules: - Always announce the caller's name, phone number, and brief issue when transferring. - If the transfer fails or no one picks up within 30 seconds, return to the caller.
integration_constraints: - We currently do not use a CRM. - Please do NOT integrate with any external ticketing tools. - Just log the call summary in your own system and send us a daily email summary manually for now.
after_hours_flow_summary: - Greet the caller, specify that they have reached the after-hours answering service, confirm if it is an emergency, and follow the emergency vs non-emergency routing rules.
office_hours_flow_summary: - Greet the caller, confirm how you can help, collect their name and phone number, determine if it is an emergency, and then either transfer or take a detailed message.
